pattern: Since the shop lets the customer nominate any eligible candy as the freebie, sorting priciest-first and skipping payment on every third candy guarantees the largest possible items get discounted.
hints: The shop lets you choose which candy to take for free, so think about ordering the purchases to make sure your most expensive candies are always the ones you actually pay for.
If you always buy the two priciest candies still available as one purchase, the next-priciest remaining candy is always a valid freebie for that pair.
Sort the costs from highest to lowest and walk through them in groups of three: pay for the first two of each group and skip the third.

edge_inputs.description: six candies, two full groups of three
exactly two candies, no third to take free
max array length, cheapest price repeated throughout
max price repeated, length divisible by three
already ascending, must still be sorted descending before grouping
duplicate cheapest values compete for the same free slot
length not divisible by three leaves a leftover pair at the end

stress.description: random costs at max array length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    cost = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [cost]
